description: Assuming content is globally fresh immediately after deploy/purge, while edge PoPs still serve the old version.
body: Failure mode: a release assumes the change is live everywhere the instant it deploys, but edge caches propagate over time, so some users keep getting stale assets — sometimes mixed old/new causing breakage. Detection signals: inconsistent behavior across regions after deploy; old asset hashes still served; support reports of stale UI. Do not treat edge propagation as instant. Safer fallback: version/fingerprint asset URLs so new content has new keys, measure purge propagation, and gate releases that require atomic switchover behind versioned paths.
